watergate-salad.name: Watergate Salad

ingredients.item: Crushed pineapple in juice (such as dole®)
Frozen whipped topping (such as cool whip®)

instructions: Mix pineapple, marshmallows, pudding mix, and pecans together in a bowl; fold in whipped topping. Refrigerate salad until chilled and thickened, about 1 hour.

watergate-salad-from-dole.name: Watergate Salad From Dole
instructions: Combine undrained pineapple and pudding mix in a large bowl. Mix in marshmallows and pecans until well blended. Gently stir in whipped topping. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving.

wheat-berry-bowl-with-merguez-and-pomegr.name: Wheat Berry Bowl With Merguez And Pomegranate

ingredients.prep: a spice blend of herbs, sumac, and sesame seeds, is available at Middle Eastern markets, specialty foods stores, and online.

instructions: Simmer wheat berries in a large saucepan of salted water until grains are tender and hulls have just started to split open (cook time will vary and can take up to 1 hour). Drain and rinse under cold water, then thoroughly drain again. Transfer to a large bowl.

Meanwhile, heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a medium skillet, preferably cast iron, over medium-high. Cook sausage, breaking up with a wooden spoon, until browned and crisp, 8–10 minutes. Transfer to a medium bowl with a slotted spoon.
Mix garlic, yogurt, 1 Tbsp. oil, 1 Tbsp. lemon juice, and 2 tsp. za’atar in a medium bowl; season with salt and pepper. Set yogurt sauce aside.
Add pomegranate seeds, chopped mint, half of scallions, remaining 2 Tbsp. oil, and remaining 1 Tbsp. lemon juice to bowl with wheat berries and toss to combine. Season with salt and pepper.
Divide wheat berry mixture among bowls and top with cucumbers, sausage, and remaining scallions. Drizzle with reserved yogurt sauce, scatter mint leaves over, and sprinkle with more za’atar.

whimpies.name: Whimpies
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat. Cook and stir celery, onion, green bell pepper, and garlic in hot oil until softened, about 5 minutes. Add ground beef, increase heat to medium-high, and stir until crumbly and browned, about 10 minutes.

Stir in ketchup, chili sauce, water, parsley, vinegar, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, salt, pepper, mustard powder, chili powder, and paprika until well combined; bring to a simmer. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.

whipped-cream-mousse-frosting.name: Whipped Cream Mousse Frosting

ingredients.item: Frozen whipped topping (such as cool whip®)

instructions: Beat whipping cream, instant pudding mix, milk, and vanilla extract together with an electric hand mixer in a large bowl until soft peaks start to form. Lift your beater or whisk straight up: the mixture will form soft mounds rather than a sharp peak.
Fold whipped topping into whipping cream mixture to evenly mix. Refrigerate until ready to use.

whole-wheat-flour-cake-with-mixed-cheese.name: Whole Wheat Flour Cake With Mixed Cheese And Pepper
whole-wheat-flour-cake-with-mixed-cheese.original_name: Mixed Cheese And Pepper Full Wheat Flour Cake
instructions: 1. Peppers backbone size andush.
2. 1 month ago add oil to the pan and slightly sauce the peppers.
3. Eggs in a deep container &reparable.
4. Sunflower add oil, yogurt and cheesesto rub.
5. Add chopped peppers.
6. Add full wheat flour, baking powder, salt and pepper.
7. Finally add the greens you're thin.
8. Transfer the material to the oven container you lubricate.
9. Öpreheated from 160 degrees to 55-60 minutes.
10. Serve after cooling.

whoopie-pies-with-german-chocolate-filli.name: Whoopie Pies With German Chocolate Filling

ingredients.item: Nonhydrogenated solid vegetable shortening or unsalted butter

instructions: Position rack in center of oven and preheat to 191°C. Line 2 large rimmed baking sheets with parchment paper. Sift flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, and baking soda into medium bowl. Using electric mixer, beat shortening, sugar, egg yolks, and vanilla in large bowl until well blended, about 2 minutes. Add flour mixture in 3 additions alternately with milk in 2 additions, beating until blended after each addition.

Drop dough by very rounded tablespoonfuls onto prepared baking sheets, spacing 3 inches apart. Bake 1 sheet at a time until cookies are slightly puffed and spread but are still soft, 8 to 10 minutes. Cool on sheets 10 minutes. Carefully transfer cookies to racks and cool completely. Repeat with remaining cookie dough. DO AHEAD Cookies can be made 1 day ahead. Store airtight in single layer at room temperature.

Whisk evaporated milk, egg yolks, and vanilla in heavy large saucepan to blend. Whisk in sugar and pinch of salt. Add butter. Stir constantly over medium heat until mixture thickens and turns golden and instant-read thermometer inserted into mixture registers 79°C to 81°C, 8 to 10 minutes (do not boil). Remove saucepan from heat, stir in coconut and pecans. Transfer German chocolate filling to bowl and cool completely, stirring occasionally. Cover and chill until filling is slightly firmer, about 1 hour. DO AHEAD Can be made 1 days ahead. Keep chilled.

Line 2 large rimmed baking sheets with parchment paper. Bring cream to simmer in heavy small saucepan. Remove from heat. Add chocolate and whisk until melted and smooth. Cool ganache until lukewarm but still spreadable, about 15 minutes.

Spread 1 rounded tablespoon German chocolate filling on bottom (flat side) of half of cookies. Place cookies, filling side up, on prepared baking sheet. Spoon 1 teaspoon chocolate ganache onto bottom (flat side) of remaining half of cookies. Place 1 ganache-topped cookie, ganache side down, atop each German chocolate-filled cookie, pressing slightly to adhere. DO AHEAD Can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and refrigerate in airtight container.

wild-asparagus-baked-or-in-the-air-fryer.name: Wild Asparagus Baked Or In The Air Fryer
wild-asparagus-baked-or-in-the-air-fryer.original_name: Espárragos trigueros al horno o en freidora de aire

ingredients.item: 100 g de queso parmesano, mozzarella o gouda

instructions: It purifies the oven to 200 been 3 been (199 been 3). Prepare the ingredients If you will make them in air fryer hot at 180 ° C for 5 minutes

Wash and clear the green asparagus. Remove the woody bottom from the stalks. Put the asparagus on a baking plate and scuba them with some olive oil. Sprinkle salt and pepper to taste and mix well so that they are uniformly covered. If they are put in an air fryer put them in a frying basket, make sure to mix them well and get them impregnated with olive oil. If you're going to do a lot of asparagus it's best to do them in tands to stop them from pounding up. Try and get them well distributed at the bottom of the basket.
Put the cheese evenly distributed onto the asparagus. You can also use grated cheese. Cut the lemon into thin slices and place them onto the asparagus.
Hornea for about 15-20 minutes, or until asparagus are tender and cheese is golden and bubbling. The time at the air fryer is similar and will have them ready in about 15 minutes.
wild-asparagus-baked-or-in-the-air-fryer.category: Fast and easy dinner, Easy home food recipes

wild-rice-and-mushroom-stuffing.name: Wild Rice And Mushroom Stuffing
instructions: Place wild rice in heavy medium saucepan. Add enough cold water to cover rice by 3 inches. Bring to boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until rice is almost tender, about 45 minutes. Drain.

Meanwhile, melt 1 tablespoon butter with 1 tablespoon oil in heavy large pot over medium-high heat. Add shiitakes; sauté until brown, about 7 minutes. Transfer to large bowl. Add 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon oil to same pot; heat over medium-high heat. Add oyster mushrooms; sauté until brown, about 5 minutes. Transfer to bowl with shiitakes.

Add 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon oil to same pot; heat over medium heat. Add onions to pot; sauté until golden and very tender, about 20 minutes. Add chile; stir 1 minute. Add remaining 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon oil to pot; add brown rice and stir 2 minutes. Add wild rice, broth, bay leaves, and 1 teaspoon coarse salt; bring to boil. Reduce heat to medium; cover and simmer until all rice is tender and liquid is absorbed, 35 to 40 minutes. Fluff rice mixture with fork. Stir in mushrooms and parsley. Season to taste with more salt and pepper. Stir briefly over medium heat until heated through. Transfer to large bowl.

woodrows-coffee-bbq-brisket-sandwich.name: Woodrow's Coffee Bbq Brisket Sandwich

ingredients.prep: cut into thin shoestrings (use a mandoline if possible)

instructions: To make the BBQ rub: Mix together smoked paprika, brown sugar, chili powder, salt, pepper, granulated garlic and onion powder in a bowl. Set aside.

Trim excess fat from the outside of the brisket, keeping about a 1/2-inch layer of fat on. Chop up the excess fat and place it in a pot with 1 cup of the vegetable oil. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until the fat starts to brown, 10 to 15 minutes. Turn off heat and let cool, then strain to remove all the fat chunks. Set aside.

Rub some of the remaining 1/4 cup vegetable oil all around the outside of the brisket. Rub brisket liberally with 3/4 cup of the prepared BBQ rub (save remainder for another use). Rub the rest of the vegetable oil on top of the BBQ rub. (Remember the order: "oil, rub, oil.")
Let the brisket rest, covered and refrigerated, overnight. If you are in a hurry, you could let it sit in the fridge for a couple hours.
Heat an outdoor grill to 177°C (175 C). Preheat an oven to 93°C (93 C).

Sear the outside of the meat on all sides on the grill. This step helps caramelize the outside and impart some smoke flavor since a smoker is not used. Cover the meat with foil or thick butcher paper and place in a hotel pan.
Transfer the pan to the oven and roast for 12 hours or overnight. Let cool.
To make the coffee BBQ sauce: Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in a saucepot, then add chopped garlic and shallots. Cook, stirring, until they start to caramelize.

Add the BBQ sauce, coffee, and bay leaf to the pot and simmer on medium-low heat, stirring frequently, for about 30 minutes. Let cool, remove the bay leaf, and puree in a blender (optional). Set aside.

To prepare for sandwiches: Slice the brisket about 1/4-inch-thick and place in a large saute pan, ensuring each piece can lie flat in a single layer. Add just enough coffee BBQ sauce to come about halfway up the side of the meat. Heat the pan on medium to high heat.
Split the burger buns. Toast the buns and add the melted butter to the insides.
Heat the oil in a deep-fryer to 177°C (175 C).

Fry shoestring potatoes until they start to turn golden brown. Then, add the sliced jalapenos to the fryer and continue frying until the fries are golden and jalapenos are crispy but still bright green, 1 to 2 minutes. Let drain, then place in a bowl and season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
While frying, turn each piece of brisket over in the pan with tongs and continue searing until a deep-dark crust forms on the outside.

Pour about 1/4 cup of the reserved beef fat into another saute pan. Heat on medium-high heat and add the sliced onions. Cook the onions down until they start to get a dark caramelization. Add salt and pepper to taste. Turn off heat, drain excess oil, and set aside.

To assemble the sandwiches: Place buns on a tray. Lay your Cheddar cheese down on the bottom bun, add the BBQ brisket on top, beef fat onions on top of the brisket, and jalapeno shoestrings on top of the onions. Place the top bun lids on. Serve immediately.

100-whole-wheat-bread.name: 100% Whole Wheat Bread
instructions: Combine warm water, honey, and yeast in the bowl of a stand mixer; let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes.
Add 4 cups whole wheat flour, olive oil, and salt. Mix on low with the dough hook until just combined.
Cover the bowl and let dough rest for 15 minutes to allow gluten to begin developing.
Knead dough on medium speed until smooth and elastic and the sides of the bowl are clean, about 7 minutes.
Cover the bowl and let dough rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
Lightly grease a 9x5-inch loaf pan. Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface, shape into a loaf, and place seam-side down in the prepared pan.
Let dough rise until it domes 1 inch above the rim of the pan, about 1 hour.
Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Bake loaf in the preheated oven until golden brown and loaf sounds hollow when tapped, 40 to 45 minutes.
Remove loaf from the pan immediately and cool completely on a wire rack before slicing.
